methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Varda Space Industries, a Series C frontier-tech company in Space & Aerospace founded by Will Bruey and Delian Asparouhov, is valued at $1.5B post-money following $131M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of 4 same-stage private comparables (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace) valued between $1.0B and $2.5B, plus 5 public-market Space & Aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median EV/Sales multiple of 53.2x.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.7x typical Series C step-up multiplier to the current $1.5B post-money valuation, implying a forward value of $2.5B. Peer-Median analysis benchmarks against the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables, which carry a median valuation of $1.5B (range: $1.0B–$2.5B), implying no premium to current valuation. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue data. 

Triangulating these two methods yields a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $2.9B (high), with a median of $2.0B. The current $1.5B valuation sits at the lower end of this range, reflecting peer-set anchoring. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory (unavailable for calibration), competitive positioning within the crowded commercial-space ecosystem, and execution risk on near-term milestones typical of Series C stage companies.
